name: Yogurt Rye Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir the warm water and yeast together until dissolved. Let stand for about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in the yogurt, 9-grain cereal, bran flakes, salt, butter, rye flour, and 1 1/2 cups of all-purpose flour. Beat vigorously to form a smooth dough.
3. Add enough all-purpose flour to make a manageable dough, then turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for several minutes until the dough is elastic.
4. Sprinkle with additional all-purpose flour as needed to prevent sticking. Place in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap or a clean damp cloth, and let rise until doubled in bulk.
5. Punch down the dough and divide into 3 equal parts. Roll each piece into a long round strand about 3/4-inch thick, ensuring all strands are the same length.
6. Pinch the strands together at one end, braid them carefully, and secure the other end under the loaf to create a neat shape. Place on a baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal.
7. Cover loosely and let rise again until doubled in bulk. In another bowl, whisk together 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon water.
8. Brush the braided dough evenly with the egg wash, then preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
9. Bake for 45 minutes, or until golden brown and firm to the touch. Remove from the sheet and cool on a rack.
Makes 1 Loaf
